title: Dehydrated Crispy Mediterranean Fregola Pilaf
description: A jammy, crispy, and lazy Sunday Mediterranean dish featuring fregola, this recipe is perfect for a relaxing afternoon, and is FODMAP friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200 degrees Fahrenheit to begin the dehydration process.
In a large bowl, toss the toasted fregola with olive oil, chopped onion, and garlic infused oil until the fregola is well coated.
Spread the fregola mixture on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper in a single layer.
Dehydrate the fregola mixture in the oven for 2 hours, stirring occasionally, until it reaches the desired crispiness.
While the fregola is dehydrating, mix the cherry tomatoes, kalamata olives, and artichoke hearts in a separate bowl.
After the fregola has finished dehydrating, add it to the bowl with the tomato and olive mixture, and stir to combine.
Season the mixture with salt and black pepper to taste.
Stir in the chopped fresh parsley.
Serve the Dehydrated Crispy Mediterranean Fregola Pilaf warm or at room temperature.
